title: Coding-agent cross-session memory
scenario: A terminal-resident coding agent persists architectural decisions across days so it can recall "what we tried Tuesday" via pgvector similarity search.

title: Ephemeral test database for a risky migration
scenario: A coding agent provisions a throwaway Postgres in under a second, dry-runs a destructive migration, inspects the result, then discards the database — no production blast radius.

title: Repo-wide code dependency graph
scenario: An agent indexes a polyrepo into a blast-radius graph so it can answer "what breaks if I rename this function" before editing.

title: PR-review bot triggered by webhooks
scenario: A code-review agent receives PR webhooks from GitHub, posts inline comments, and re-reviews on each push while caching diffs between runs.

title: Sandboxed test runner per task
scenario: A coding agent spins up a throwaway container per task to run the user's test suite in isolation, tears it down on success or failure.

title: Tool-call rate-limit and budget cache
scenario: A coding agent caches LLM token budgets and rate-limit windows in Redis so parallel sub-agents don't burn quota fighting each other.

title: Conversation transcript archive
scenario: Coding sessions are persisted as JSONL transcripts for replay, fine-tuning, and "what did we try last sprint" search across the team.

title: Multi-repo shared scratchpad
scenario: Multiple agents working across separate repos coordinate via a shared scratchpad document store keyed by feature branch.

title: LangGraph state checkpoints
scenario: A LangGraph workflow checkpoints node state to Postgres so a crashed run resumes mid-graph instead of restarting from scratch.

title: CrewAI message bus fan-out
scenario: A crew of planner / retriever / critic agents publishes tasks on NATS subjects so any worker pod can pull and reply.

title: AutoGen group-chat history
scenario: An AutoGen multi-agent chat stores per-conversation message logs in Mongo for audit and replay across days.

title: Shared episodic memory store
scenario: A planner and a researcher agent read and write the same episodic memory table so each agent sees the other's findings.

title: Durable agent task queue
scenario: A supervisor agent enqueues sub-tasks on a durable queue; failed jobs re-emerge with exponential backoff.

title: Cross-framework A2A gateway
scenario: A gateway service translates A2A-protocol messages between a LangGraph crew and a CrewAI crew over pub/sub.

title: Live agent status broadcast
scenario: Worker agents broadcast heartbeats on a status subject so a dashboard shows which agent is stuck mid-tool-call.

title: Clinical-scribe note storage
scenario: A medical scribe agent transcribes doctor-patient visits and stores structured SOAP notes per encounter with auditable history.

title: Personal-injury demand letters
scenario: A legal agent drafts demand letters from case files, stores prior versions, and emits webhooks to the firm's case-management system.

title: AML transaction monitor
scenario: A finance agent ingests transaction streams, flags suspicious patterns, and persists compliance decisions with full reasoning trace.

title: Adaptive-tutoring student model
scenario: A tutor agent tracks per-student concept mastery, picks the next problem, and adjusts difficulty based on response patterns.

title: Contract redline cache
scenario: A contract-review agent caches clause embeddings so re-running redlines on a 200-page MSA is instant on the second pass.

title: EHR appointment webhook fan-in
scenario: A medical-intake agent receives appointment-created webhooks from multiple EHR vendors and unifies them into one queue.

title: Obsidian-vault embedding sync
scenario: A personal research agent embeds Obsidian notes nightly into pgvector and answers questions across years of writing.

title: Cross-device chat history
scenario: A personal assistant stores conversation history in Mongo so the same agent picks up context on phone, laptop, and watch.

title: Daily-journal episodic memory
scenario: An assistant logs each day as a structured journal entry and retrieves "what was I working on Monday?" with sub-second latency.

title: arXiv-and-RSS research feed
scenario: A research agent receives webhook pings from arXiv and RSS bridges, dedupes via Redis, and stores PDFs for later retrieval.

title: Voice-memo capture pipeline
scenario: A second-brain agent receives uploaded voice memos, transcribes them, and files transcripts plus audio for semantic search.

title: CRM for one person
scenario: A personal CRM agent remembers names, birthdays, and last-conversation summaries indexed per contact.

title: Browser-session cookie store
scenario: A browser agent persists login cookies and session state per target site so it doesn't re-auth on every run.

title: Scraped product-price history
scenario: A shopping-watcher agent scrapes prices hourly and stores time-series rows for "alert me when it drops 20%".

title: Screenshot evidence archive
scenario: A QA agent captures before/after screenshots on every test run and stores them keyed by run-id for diff review.

title: Browser job queue with retries
scenario: A fleet of Playwright workers pulls navigation tasks from a queue, marks them done, and retries on captcha.

title: Form-fill state machine
scenario: A long-running form-completion agent persists field-by-field progress so a captcha pause doesn't lose 30 minutes of work.

title: Accessibility-tree selector cache
scenario: A scraping agent caches a11y-tree snapshots in Redis so repeat selectors resolve in single-digit milliseconds.

title: Full dev backend in one curl
scenario: An AI agent or developer provisions Postgres + Redis + MongoDB anonymously to develop against — no Docker, no cloud account, no installer — and tears it down when done.

title: Pre-commit skill-scanner webhook
scenario: An MCP skill security scanner receives pre-commit webhooks, scans agent skills against a ruleset, and blocks the push on critical findings.

title: SARIF scan-result store
scenario: A security scanner posts SARIF results into Postgres so trend dashboards show drift over weeks of commits.

title: Deploy-status MCP server
scenario: An MCP server tracks live deploy status across environments and pushes updates to a chat agent over pub/sub.

title: CI flake-tracker
scenario: A CI bot ingests test-run webhooks, fingerprints failures, and surfaces "this test is flaky 30% of runs".

title: High-volume PR-review pipeline
scenario: An automated reviewer handles thousands of MRs/day, queues each review job, and stores comment artifacts per run.

title: On-call incident-response agent
scenario: An on-call agent listens for alert webhooks, executes a runbook, and posts the action log back into the incident ticket.

title: A2A agent-card registry
scenario: A marketplace stores Agent Cards (skills, tags, pricing) and serves discovery queries to client agents.

title: x402 micropayment ledger
scenario: An agent-payment hub records x402 micropayments between agents and reconciles balances per principal.

title: Agent reputation log
scenario: Buyer agents leave ratings on seller agents after each tool call; reputation scores aggregate hourly.

title: AP2 mandate audit trail
scenario: An agentic-commerce gateway stores signed user mandates ("buy X up to $Y") for later dispute resolution.

title: Cross-agent shared inbox
scenario: Two agents negotiate over a shared subject; messages persist in JetStream so neither needs to be online simultaneously.

title: Agent-marketplace preview thumbnails
scenario: An agent marketplace stores screenshot previews of each listed agent's UI for human browsing.

title: One-afternoon MVP backend
scenario: A solo founder spins up Postgres + Redis from a curl call in their Claude Code session and ships a paid product by evening.

title: Pre-launch waitlist store
scenario: A landing page captures emails via a webhook receiver and stores them with UTM context for nurture campaigns.

title: Stripe-event entitlements
scenario: A SaaS receives Stripe webhooks, updates user plan tier, and invalidates a Redis entitlement cache atomically.

title: Solo-founder analytics warehouse
scenario: A founder pipes product events into Postgres and runs SQL queries via an MCP-Postgres tool from chat.

title: Side-project container deploy
scenario: An indie hacker ships a Dockerized app to a subdomain with one HTTP call — no DevOps account, no Helm chart, no IaC.

title: 24-hour hackathon backend
scenario: A team provisions Postgres + Mongo + MinIO anonymously, ships their demo, and lets the stack expire 24 hours after judging.

title: Classroom-per-student sandbox
scenario: A CS professor provisions one ephemeral Postgres per student for a SQL assignment, dropped after grading.

title: Agent-resilience chaos lab
scenario: A research hackathon tests how agents behave when their database, cache, and message bus randomly fail mid-task.

title: LangGraph fan-out research agents
scenario: A planner agent splits a research question into 12 sub-queries and dispatches them to parallel LangGraph workers that each hit different sources, returning JSON to a NATS subject the reducer subscribes to.

title: Claude Code agent-teams scratchpad
scenario: A lead Claude Code session spawns 6 sibling worker sessions, each owning one module of a monorepo; they coordinate by reading and writing a shared scratchpad table.

title: OpenAI Agents SDK handoff mesh
scenario: A triage agent hands off to specialist agents (refund, shipping, fraud) running concurrently, each maintaining its own conversation thread row keyed by handoff_id.

title: Magentic-One DAG executor
scenario: A Microsoft-Magentic-One-style orchestrator decomposes a goal into a task DAG, dispatches independent branches to parallel agents, and merges their outputs after a quality-gate agent approves each branch.

title: Scatter-gather price comparison swarm
scenario: A consumer agent broadcasts a product query to 40 retailer-specific shopper agents over a NATS subject; the first 5 cheapest replies win and the rest are cancelled mid-flight.

title: CrewAI parallel-process crew
scenario: A CrewAI Process.parallel run executes researcher, writer, and fact-checker agents simultaneously against a shared pgvector store, each persisting partial drafts as it works.

title: Copy-on-write Postgres for agent migrations
scenario: An Ardent-style coding agent forks a 6-second copy-on-write clone of prod Postgres, runs the candidate ALTER TABLE, validates row counts, then destroys the clone.

title: E2B microVM sandbox per agent turn
scenario: Each turn of a code-writing agent spins up a fresh E2B-style microVM whose scratch Postgres connection_url lives only for the lifetime of the sandbox, then is reaped.

title: Daytona warm-pool data workspace
scenario: A Daytona warm pool of 50 Python sandboxes each pre-attaches to a per-sandbox Redis namespace for caching pip-resolver results, so cold start to first agent action is under 100ms.

title: Replit-Agent preview backend
scenario: A Replit-Agent-style preview deploys a one-shot app with a 24h-TTL Postgres so a reviewer can poke at the prototype, then everything is collected when the share link expires.

title: Cursor background-agent worktree
scenario: A Cursor background agent claims an isolated dev environment for each branch it's working on, with its own Redis for build caches and a webhook to ping the IDE when the PR is ready.

title: Sandbox-per-PR preview deployment
scenario: A reviewer agent provisions a throwaway storage bucket plus Postgres tied to a PR number, seeds it from a snapshot, and the whole thing is torn down when the PR closes.

title: Cloudflare sub-agent factory per user
scenario: A Durable-Object-style root agent mints a fresh sub-agent for each end-user, each with its own SQL-backed memory and a deploy slot for that user's tools.

title: AgentCore tenant-scoped spawning
scenario: An AWS-AgentCore-style multi-tenant SaaS spins up one isolated agent runtime per customer org on demand, each pulling its own connection_url from the platform's vault.

title: Smithery one-MCP-per-skill mint
scenario: A Smithery-like MCP host launches a dedicated MCP server per installed skill behind a webhook URL, each receiving tool calls and writing results to a shared Redis bus.

title: AI cofounder spawns a startup
scenario: A founder-agent reads a one-line idea, provisions Postgres + MinIO + a webhook + a deploy slot, generates a landing page, and hands the bundle to a marketing sub-agent.

title: Per-tenant chatbot factory at signup
scenario: When a B2B SaaS user clicks 'Add AI assistant', the factory provisions a dedicated Mongo for that tenant's chat history, a Redis for session state, and deploys an isolated agent container.

title: Devin-style PR-bot fleet
scenario: A Cognition-Devin-style orchestrator spawns one fresh agent worker per inbound GitHub issue, each with its own scratch Postgres for running migrations against, and reports back via webhook.

title: Anthropic parallel tool_use batch
scenario: A Claude turn emits 8 simultaneous tool_use blocks; an executor fans them out to 8 NATS subjects, each handler writes its result to Redis keyed by tool_use_id, and the assistant collects them for the next turn.

title: Browser-agent action pool
scenario: A Skyvern-style planner emits 20 actions (fill, click, scroll) that fan out across 20 Browserbase tabs simultaneously; results aggregate in Mongo keyed by step_id.

title: Parallel SQL-plan probe
scenario: A data-analyst agent issues 12 candidate EXPLAIN ANALYZE queries against a forked Postgres in parallel, picking the lowest-cost plan to run against the real DB.

title: Multi-model bake-off router
scenario: A router agent dispatches the same prompt to GPT, Claude, and Gemini concurrently; the first valid JSON response wins, and loser turns get cached in Redis for next time.

title: Speculative agent rollout race
scenario: A speculative-decoding-style orchestrator runs the same task at three temperatures in parallel; a verifier agent picks the best output and discards the rest, all coordinated via NATS request/reply.

title: Pyodide notebook cell agent farm
scenario: An analyst agent splits a 200-cell notebook across 30 Pyodide workers, each writing intermediate dataframes to MinIO so the planner can pull only the ones it needs.

title: OpenTelemetry agent-trace ingest
scenario: A Langfuse-style collector receives OTel spans from 200 concurrent agents via a webhook endpoint, writes them to Postgres, and stores large prompt payloads in MinIO.

title: Per-agent dead-letter inspection queue
scenario: Every failed tool call from a swarm is republished to a NATS DLQ; an investigator agent pulls them in batches, classifies the failure mode, and persists clusters in Mongo for the operator UI.

title: Live agent-topology graph
scenario: Each spawn/handoff event is published to a NATS subject; a topology agent subscribes and maintains a real-time graph of which parent spawned which child in Postgres for the dashboard.

title: Token-cost ledger per sub-agent
scenario: Every LLM call from any agent in a fleet emits a webhook with input/output tokens; a ledger agent aggregates spend per tenant in Postgres and triggers throttling at thresholds.

title: Cross-agent replay debugger
scenario: A debug agent stores full prompt+response payloads for an entire multi-agent run in MinIO, with an index in Mongo so an engineer can replay any branch deterministically.

title: Agent-run lineage store
scenario: An evaluation team builds a lineage UI where every run links to its parent run and its eval verdict; the relation graph lives in Postgres and the raw transcripts in MinIO.

title: x402 micropayment per tool call
scenario: An agent calling another agent's premium MCP tool receives HTTP 402, settles a 0.3-cent USDC payment, and retries; the receiver tracks paid calls in Redis with a Postgres ledger.

title: AP2 mandate broker
scenario: A Google-AP2-style mandate service stores a user's shopping mandate in Postgres, lets buyer agents request approval via webhook, and emits signed receipts for each purchase.

title: Agent-marketplace escrow
scenario: A platform lets agents post jobs and other agents bid; funds sit in an escrow row in Postgres and release via webhook once a verifier agent signs off on delivery.

title: Per-agent rate-limited API key vault
scenario: A vault service mints scoped, rate-limited API keys for child agents on demand; Redis tracks per-key usage and the spend rolls up to a Postgres billing event stream.

title: Stripe-ACP checkout agent
scenario: A shopping agent uses the Stripe + OpenAI Agentic Commerce Protocol to place an order; the merchant agent receives a webhook with the cart, persists it in Mongo, and ships.

title: SWE-bench parallel rollout harness
scenario: A benchmark runner spawns 500 isolated agent instances against 500 SWE-bench-Verified tasks, each with a private Postgres scratch and its results written to MinIO for the judge model.

title: GAIA tournament bracket
scenario: A tournament service runs 16 agent variants head-to-head on GAIA tasks; pairings live in Mongo, intermediate transcripts in MinIO, win counts in Redis sorted sets for the live leaderboard.

title: Terminal-Bench shell sandbox grid
scenario: A grid runner deploys 100 ephemeral shell sandboxes, each running a Terminal-Bench task with its own ephemeral DB; pass/fail webhooks aggregate to a Postgres results table.

title: Adversarial red-team runner
scenario: A LangWatch-Scenario-style service spawns attacker agents that probe a target chatbot in parallel; transcripts persist in Mongo and successful jailbreaks fire a webhook to the security team.

title: LLM-as-judge consensus pool
scenario: Five judge agents score the same agent output in parallel; their verdicts converge in Postgres and a tiebreaker rolls in only on disagreement, all coordinated through a NATS request/reply.

title: Trajectory diff regression harness
scenario: On every PR to the agent repo, a CI agent re-runs 1000 cached trajectories in parallel and stores diffs against the baseline in MinIO so reviewers see exactly which behaviors changed.

title: Inbox-zero agent fleet
scenario: A Cloudflare-Email-style fleet runs one durable agent per user that triages incoming email, files attachments in MinIO, and stores extracted action items in Postgres.

title: Cron-scheduled scraping swarm
scenario: A scheduler fans out 5000 cron-triggered scraper agents per hour; each writes its diff to Mongo and pings a webhook only when the page changed.

title: Long-horizon Temporal agent workflow
scenario: A Temporal-backed agent runs a 14-day procurement negotiation across vendors; each step persists in Postgres with NATS signals so a human can interject without breaking the workflow.

title: Restate-style durable sidecar
scenario: A Restate sidecar intercepts agent HTTP tool calls and journals them to Postgres so any crash mid-run resumes deterministically without re-charging external APIs.

title: Overnight dossier fleet
scenario: An async research service queues hundreds of overnight dossier jobs; workers persist intermediate findings in Mongo and PDF outputs in MinIO when complete.

title: Slack/Discord async bot factory
scenario: A factory spins up one durable agent per Slack workspace that subscribes to events via webhook, batches them, and runs nightly summarizer jobs persisting digests in Postgres.

title: Per-device edge-agent state sync
scenario: An IoT fleet of 10,000 edge agents each runs on a Workers-style runtime, syncing local SQLite deltas to a central Postgres via webhook batches every minute.

title: Federated-learning aggregator
scenario: Edge agents on user devices compute private gradients and POST them to an aggregator webhook; the aggregator stores rounds in MinIO and the new global weights in Postgres.

title: Geo-sharded chat-agent fleet
scenario: Region-pinned agents in EU, US, and APAC each own a local Redis for session state, replicating only consented memories to a central Postgres for cross-region handoff.

_comment: Adding a new use case = one entry above. The dashboard's UseCasesPage groups by category and renders services as tags. Service codes: pg, redis, mongo, nats, minio, webhook, deploy. New categories can be added freely (the dashboard's Category type widened in PR #13).
